title: On the Nature of Obsession
author: From: Moby-Dick by Herman Melville
content: All that most maddens and torments; all that stirs up the lees of things; all truth with malice in it; all that cracks the sinews and cakes the brain; all the subtle demonisms of life and thought are visible to the driven mind.
action: Action: Reflect on whether your current drive is fueled by purpose or by external pressure.

title: The Curse of Comparison
author: From: The Geometry of Wealth
content: Comparison is the thief of joy. Measure your progress against your previous self, not someone else's current state.
action: Action: List one way your current abilities exceed your abilities from a year ago.

title: The Paradox of Choice
author: From: The Paradox of Choice
content: More options do not lead to more freedom; they lead to more anxiety and paralysis.
action: Action: Simplify your decision-making process by pre-selecting limited options.

title: The Power of Ritual
author: From: Daily Rituals
content: Great work is rarely done in bursts of genius; it is done through consistent, disciplined rituals.
action: Action: Define one 15-minute daily ritual that anchors your productivity.

title: Bias Toward Action
author: From: The Lean Startup
content: Analysis paralysis is a failure mode. Small, imperfect actions often provide better data than months of perfect planning.
action: Action: Take the smallest possible step toward a project currently stuck in 'planning' mode.
